channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jimmy Carter to receive state funeral in DC before Georgia burial
item.description: President Joe Biden, who was the first sitting senator to endorse Carter's 1976 presidential campaign, will deliver a eulogy.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, WGN 9

item.title: More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, Gaza's Health Ministry said Thursday, with no end in sight to the 15-month conflict. The ministry said a total of 46,006 Palestinians have been killed and 109,378 wounded.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lawyers for accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed battle to let his guilty plea go forward
item.description: Lawyers for the accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed are urging a federal appeals panel to let his scheduled guilty plea in the attacks go forward.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New raging inferno engulfs Hollywood's iconic epicenter and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter will be honored at Washington funeral before burial in Georgia hometown
item.description: Former U.S. President Jimmy Carter will be honored with the pageantry of a funeral in the nation’s capital and second service in his tiny Georgia hometown that launched a Depression-era farm boy to the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harrowing escapes from Los Angeles wildfires were made by foot, by car or by the grace of strangers
item.description: EDITOR'S NOTE: Click HERE to view The Associated Press' wildfire tracker, which shows where wildfires are spreading. PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Flames and pillars of smoke rose from both sides of the road and a woman yelled in panic as firefighters ushered a crowd of fleeing residents along.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, BEATRICE DUPUY, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, GENE JOHNSON and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Firefighters battle devastating Los Angeles wildfires as winds calm somewhat
item.description: Firefighters are battling to control a series of major fires in the Los Angeles area that have killed five people, ravaged communities from the Pacific Coast to Pasadena and sent thousands of people frantically fleeing their homes.
item.dc:creator: Manuel Valdes, Hallie Golden And Julie Watson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jimmy Carter lies in state at Capitol Rotunda, Trump among those paying respect
item.description: President Jimmy Carter will lie in state for a second day Wednesday after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hollywood Hills fire breaks out as deadly wildfires burn out of control across Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A fast-moving fire broke out in the Hollywood Hills on Wednesday night, threatening one of Los Angeles' most iconic spots as firefighters battled to get under control three other major blazes that killed five people, put 130,000 people under evacuation orders and ravaged communiti
item.dc:creator: MANUEL VALDES, HALLIE GOLDEN and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire hydrants ran dry in Southern California just when they were needed most
item.description: The water system used to fight the Palisades fire in Los Angeles buckled under the demands of what turned out to be the most destructive fire in city history, with some hydrants running dry as they were overstressed without assistance from firefighting aircraft for hours early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Silent on arrival: LA Mayor Karen Bass refuses to answer questions for her absence as wildfires ravage city
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass refused to answer a question after being pressed on her initial absence as wildfires raged across her jurisdiction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Space satellites track astonishing expansion of deadly California wildfires
item.description: Space satellites tracked the flames and smoke caused by eight California wildfires currently scorching the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wildfires burning in the Los Angeles area visible from space
item.description: Satellite images from NOAA and CIRA show the Palisades and Eaton Fires burning in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, WGN 9

item.title: US will keep protecting more than 2,000 Rocky Mountain grizzly bears
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — The Biden administration said Wednesday it will continue protecting about 2,000 grizzly bears in the Rocky Mountains despite opposition from Republican-led states. Federal officials also said they would ease some restrictions as conflicts between bears and humans rise.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana man killed girlfriend's 4-month-old puppy: Docs
item.description: Caleb B. Haymaker, 20, was arrested this week on one count of cruelty to an animal — a Level 6 felony.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaways on planes and inside landing gear raise worries about aviation security
item.description: What is going on with aviation security? People have been found dead hiding in the wheel wells of planes twice in the past month. Two stowaways were arrested on different flights in November and December. Then a passenger opened an emergency door while a plane was taxiing in Boston Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Reporter douses flames endangering California home on live TV
item.description: While covering one of the multiple fires currently ablaze in California, one reporter — a former EMT and volunteer firefighter — stepped in to stop flames from spreading to a home.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Convicted murderer Alex Murdaugh ordered to pay $14.8M for insurance scheme
item.description: A judge ordered Murdaugh to pay Nautilus Insurance $14.8 million for an insurance scheme involving the family of his former housekeeper.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Davis, NewsNation

item.title: Indiana man killed girlfriend's 4-month-old puppy: Docs
item.description: An Indiana man faces a felony charge after police say he threw his girlfriend's dog onto the ground in frustration, killing the 4-month-old puppy.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump asks the Supreme Court to block sentencing in his hush money case in New York
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is asking the Supreme Court to call off Friday's sentencing in his hush money case in New York. Trump's lawyers turned to the nation's highest court on Wednesday after New York courts refused to postpone the sentencing by Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter's life intersected with slavery's legacy. His record on Civil Rights is complicated
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Young Jimmy Carter and his friends were walking across a pasture after a day’s farm labor during the Great Depression. As they came to a gate, his companions stood aside and let Carter enter first. This was no act of kindness or intuitive deference to a future U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The polar vortex readies to dump snow on Texas and its neighbors
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — An area stretching from Texas to Tennessee braced Wednesday for the expected arrival of freezing rain and snow, as some other parts of the country that already received an arctic blast this week prepared to go another round with the plunging polar vortex.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A new book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team an early look at town hall questions
item.description: A forthcoming book alleges that someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump’s presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ex-police officer and Proud Boys member gets 14 months in prison for joining Capitol attack
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A former police officer who stormed the U.S. Capitol with fellow members of the far-right Proud Boys extremist group was sentenced on Wednesday to 14 months in prison for joining the Jan. 6, 2021, attack by a mob of Donald Trump supporters, court records show.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikToker whose speeding BMW killed 6 farmworkers avoids possible 55-year prison sentence with plea deal
item.description: Noah Galle reached a plea deal with prosecutors in which he was sentenced to 12 and a half years in prison after he pleaded guilty to six counts of vehicular homicide for a car crash that left six people dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Virginia elections signal no major shift in voter sentiment in a state watched closely for clues
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — There were no surprises in Virginia’s three special elections this week, but that won't stop political observers from looking for clues about voter sentiment in the first official election since President Donald Trump's sweeping victories in November.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden celebrates great-grandchild, takes no questions at California wildfire briefing with Newsom
item.description: President Biden made some remarks celebrating his great-grandchild while in California following a wildfire briefing. Neither he nor Gov. Gavin Newsom took media questions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jingle! contract workers say they're still waiting to get paid
item.description: Joe and Nikol Straws, two popular DJs from Wyandotte County, performed at Jingle! in Kansas City, Kansas, and said they're owed $18,600.
item.dc:creator: Sean McDowell, NewsNation

item.title: United Airlines passenger that landed at LAX recalls harrowing bird's-eye view of raging wildfire
item.description: A United Airlines passenger recalled the moment he caught a bird's-eye view of the raging wildfires engulfing the Los Angeles area, describing the sight as "Mordor from ‘Lord of the Rings’.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DeSantis, longtime Newsom rival, offers help to California amid wildfires
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) offered assistance to California on Wednesday as the state grapples with severe wildfires in the greater Los Angeles area. “Our prayers are with everyone affected by the horrific fires in Southern California,” DeSantis said in a post on social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter lies in state at Capitol Rotunda, Trump among those paying respect
item.description: President Jimmy Carter will lie in state for a second day Wednesday after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Military doctor pleads guilty to dozens of sexual abuse charges
item.description: A military doctor has pleaded guilty to sexually abusing dozens of soldiers at Joint Base Lewis-McChord who had primarily sought his care for pain management. Maj.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's woodworking, painting and poetry reveal an introspective Renaissance Man
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — The world knew Jimmy Carter as a president and humanitarian, but he also was a woodworker, painter and poet, creating a body of artistic work that reflects deeply personal views of the global community — and himself.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Small-town police chief ties 'Gone Girl' kidnapper to more cases after making him a pen pal
item.description: Matthew Muller, a convicted rapist in federal prison, is now charged with abducting 2 men and a woman and forcing them to pay their own ransom in 2015.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driver in Kansas City viral video explains why she jumped out of car
item.description: The Kansas City woman seen in a viral video where she ditched her car while it slid on black ice recounts what happened, and how the frightening moment led to a heartwarming ending
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, ABC 4
